review_text: Late on getting back to u

I have been denied disability twice now. I called the firm for advice and help. After a series of about 4 to 5 questions, the woman on the phone told me that I was not going to be able to receive disability because I claim an income. My income is less than 12,000 a year. I was informed that you cannot claim any kind of income if you want to be approved for disability and she hung up on me.
owner_response: We sincerely apologize for your recent experience with our firm. We understand how challenging and disheartening it can be to navigate the complexities of disability claims, especially after facing denials. Regarding our policy, we must clarify that our firm typically does not take cases where the claimants are currently working. This policy stems from our historical data showing a significantly lower success rate in cases where clients have ongoing employment. Although the government allows part-time work while receiving Social Security benefits, our track record in these scenarios has not been favorable. We regret if this policy and its implications were not communicated to you empathetically and clearly. Our goal is always to provide the best possible guidance, even when we cannot take on a case. If you have further questions or need more detailed explanations, please don’t hesitate to contact Shawn, who will be more than willing to assist you. Again, we are sorry for any frustration or inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding of our position. Sincerely, Shawn Good The Good Law Group

review_text: I urge you not to use this group for they are a very crooked law office that plays game with the system at the expense of their clients. They were not honest. On the day of my hearing the most important date, Mr Good senior sends his son to represent me, his son makes me sign a new client form to allow him to represent me. Of course I sign with his son out of desperation because my hearing is in 10 minutes. His son Good jr acted very crass towards the judge and ultimately ended up threatening the judge by saying I don't care what decision you make we will appeal! I signed up with Mr Good senior, someone I thought was an honest experienced attorney and he completely let me down and sent his inexperienced son to represent me at my hearing last minute! Again go with the bigger groups you see on TV... this law group is no good!
owner_response: We regret to hear that you are displeased with your recent experience with us. We appreciate you bringing this experience to our attention. -Shawn

review_text: Call these guys if you want someone to tell you to do it yourself and they'll file the papers. Their phone assistant was hired to tell people that they should go on the SS website. Way to go. What you're saying is you won't help unless it's been figured out already. What if my disability doesn't allow me to do such things? Call elsewhere.
owner_response: We are sorry we could not help you. Without the information we need regarding eligibility for benefits there is nothing we can do. If you want to call me directly, please call my office at 847/577-4476 and ask for Shawn.

review_text: They do not return calls. After my 4th call to inquire status of my case the receptionist said she was told to tell me I was too old for them to represent me in a disability case.
owner_response: We are sorry you think we did not return your calls. We pride ourselves on being highly responsive so this feedback is troubling. Generally, if you are at or above full retirement age you are not eligible for benefits. This is not our rules, it is federal law. -Shawn

review_text: Shawn was very friendly and also very helpful in getting me to and from the court hearing. In my current situation, I would have not had been able to get there otherwise.

If you want to pay someone $3,500 just to be screamed at and thrown out of their office, then this is the lawyer for you. I hired him to handle my case, but during what should have been a routine office visit, he lost his temper simply because I was wearing a beer T-shirt under a zipped hoodie. When I calmly told him I didn’t appreciate being spoken to that way—especially after paying him thousands of dollars—he threw my folder across the desk, told me it was “16 hours of work” and that I wouldn’t be getting anything back, then ordered his secretary to kick me out. I never received a refund, never got proper legal help from him, and ultimately had to hire another attorney (Albert Altamore) to fix the mess. This incident happened about a year ago, and I waited until all my court matters were resolved before posting this review to make sure he couldn’t jeopardize my case. I’m writing this now to warn others: save your money, time, and peace of mind.
owner_response: Adrianna, thank you for your feedback. The attorney that handled your case is no longer with the firm. We strive to provide only the best representation for all of our clients. If the need ever arises, we hope you would you would consider contacting us for a free consultation.

review_text: Divorces are messy even if you don’t want them to be! Having a paralegal like Maria makes a huge difference. She GETS it! And I knew she was in my corner all the way! Mason was great and he could not do what he does without a team behind him. It was an annoying, frustrating process but the Crosby team did what they could within the confines of the law. Unfortunately, laws can hamstring alot of things but Mason and Maira did a good job of guiding where it matters most. Thanks to you both!

The process of my divorce took 5 months. You will mostly be working with the paralegals which are not friendly at all. It’s an inconvenience if you call or email. Don’t expect fast responses. Absolutely no compassion either. Divorce is a difficult process but you are on your own. I had to remind them of all the demands I had. They even missed a few demands that I had required. I felt like an inconvenience to them. Mason & Tyler are kind. But they need better paralegals. I’m just glad this is over with.

If I could give 6 stars, I would. I made a terrible mistake and was charged with a DUI. I’ve never been in trouble with the law, not even a ticket, so my anxiety was eating me alive. I came across Paul and, after reading all of the reviews, I chose him to represent me in court, and it was probably one of the best decisions I made. Not only did he reassure me when it came to anxiety, but he was hands-on every step of the way. Every time I had a question or concern about anything, he responded quickly, whether it was through text or call. He fought for me until the very end and got me the best possible outcome. He was able to get my license suspension lifted for me with having to take a few treatment classes, court supervision, and pay fines, so if you’re ever in need of an attorney, whatever the case may be, Paul is your guy to go to. Thank you again, Paul, you’re the best!

I had a great experience with The Law Office of Paul M. Marriett. They handled my Rockford CDL ticket efficiently and kept me informed throughout the entire process. No stress, no confusion, and everything was resolved just like they said it would be. Professional, reliable, and worth it. I would definitely recommend them to anyone needing representation.

The attorney took on my case, worked it for over a month then “realized “ he couldn’t anymore bc the arresting officer was his cousin. Not sure how he didn’t know this before because he had the police report, but then he charged me the full amount and I was stuck without legal council

Had a similar experience to the other 1 start review. Called for a consult/information. Was forwarded to one of the attorneys from the front desk. Not sure the gentleman’s name, but right of the rip he was extremely rude and condescending. Barely gave me any opportunity to speak or explain my situation, only heard what he wanted to hear and then would cut me off and start rambling. Almost feel like this gentleman just enjoyed hearing his own voice. Will never attempt to go through this lawfirm again and would not recommend anybody else to try them. The biggest kick is that this gentleman casually threw in my face that the amount of money I was seeking legal action for was basically chump change and not worth his time. Well not everybody makes big lawyer money like you my friend, for some people that amount of money could genuinely be life changing.
Pros: Ryan Varner is a pretty good guy. Cons: The office staff is completely inept at their job.

I have great reservations about not awarding the firm five stars. Thus far, the representation has been outstanding. The lawyers are caring, intelligent, know the law, and are aggressive in representing my interests, and I am sure, the interests of all there clients. That the litigation is more is expensive than I, and I am sure they, anticipated is because the circumstances so unusual that they aren't likely to happen again anytime soon and it would have happened whatever law firm I retained. That said, trying to deal with the billing department is a nightmare. That is not to say that any of the enumerated costs in the billing statements I have received are out of the ordinary--if anything certain things appear to be billed somewhat conservatively: in my experience, O'Flaherty law compares favorably with the standard rates for much smaller firms that do not have nearly the same resources and experience. However, dealing with the billing department is a nightmare. Once, I thought a retainer had been paid, and months later I was notified it was not and that representation would be put on hold. Sometimes, a retainer refill will be $5,000, sometimes $10,000, and the discrepancy is never explained. Very shortly after paying the 10,000 I got a letter saying "your retainer has been depleted and is insufficient to cover a past-due balance," and asked for $5,0000. I was shocked, asked about it, and was told that it had not been depleted, that the 5,000 was essentially to get the trust back up to 10,000 but that the matter had been placed on hold until this was paid. Yet the due date to pay it was seven days later making me wonder if the matter had been placed on hold or would be placed on hold in seven days. (Aren't lawyers supposed to be precise about these things?) Nor does it seem had my lawyer paid any heed (he was more worried about my case than the billing department and there was still money in the trust.) and a couple of weeks later while considering how I would pay the retainer, I got a past due balance for a much smaller amount (under 2,000 dollars), but nowhere did it say the matter would be placed on hold until the outstanding balance was paid which perhaps would have been the most sensible place to take such action. Aren't lawyers supposed to be precise with language? Again, as far as I can tell there is nothing enumerated in the invoices that I can see which isn't relevant, necessary, or prudent in regards to the litigation, and when enumerated is billed at a very reasonable rate for a lawyer. The problem is that what the billing department says doesn't make any sense and when you ask for clarification, they respond with "oh what it says isn't true." Also, the enumerated hours come in the mail, and unsurprisingly will come at completely different time then email requests for a retainer to be refilled or a past due balance to be paid. It might be helpful it there were a secure online portal where one could examine the amount paid, the expenses billed, the retainer, any unpaid balances. I also asked the billing department how much the case had cost in total, but I didn't receive a response. (I've asked again, and I'm sure I will.) This is not over-billing or anything sinister, but it is a mess.

I had a consultation with Mr. Oancea regarding a business Small Claims Court case in Cook County, and I’m very grateful for his guidance. He carefully reviewed our case, explained the process step by step, and clearly outlined what we could realistically expect. What stood out most was his honesty — he did not make unrealistic promises, but instead gave straightforward, practical advice based on experience. I truly felt that he cared and wanted to help. I highly recommend Iurie Oancea as an attorney in Cook County. Thank you for your time and professionalism. I truly appreciate your honesty, guidance, and support.

I reached out to the attorney for a few legal clarifications and I was very satisfied with the service. He answered all of my questions patiently and provided the necessary information with clarity and professionalism. I truly appreciated his helpful attitude and clear explanations. I would definitely recommend his services to anyone who needs reliable legal advice
I really appreciate both attorneys they both did an amazing job on my husband’s case. He’s home and we thank both of them. Alex is a beast, the Judge agreed with everything he said.

These guys are horrible. First court date the Lawyer showed up late didn’t even know he had to come. Second court date came around another lawyer showed up and all she did was fix the other lawyers promblems that he had done. They sent me go another courtroom I wasn’t suppose to go to. The lawyers were extremely rude and one actually racist saying that “ oh I deal with your kind all the time” like seriously?! This was my first time having to get a lawyer and had to deal with these scums. Nothing but wasting my time and money. They call themselves lawyers but they are just idiot with certificates.

Where do I start with this review. We were referred to McNamee & Maheney Ltd. by a close friend and contacted them right away, gave our deposit and we never heard from them for over a year. We tried and tried to contact the office and always got told that our message was left for our lawyer, come to find out he did not even work there anymore and our case was forgotten about. We tried for another couple months to get answers and got nothing. We finally got an appointment, when we showed up they said the lawyer was on vacation and we still could not get our deposit back, and they would send a check in the mail. Two weeks went by and I called to see if it was in the mail, and they had not sent it and would put in directly in our account, finally three days later it was there. So bottom line if you want to get ignored and forgot about go with McNamee & Mahoney, but save your money and find another lawyer!
